title: Ukrainian Long-Range Drone Strikes on Russian Oil Infrastructure: Diplomatic Communications with European Allies; U.S. Policy Deliberations (January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2025)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I hereby request copies of all records in the possession of the Department of State related to Ukraine's campaign of long-range drone strikes against Russian oil refining and export infrastructure, and specifically: (1) diplomatic communications between the United States and European allies regarding concerns that such strikes could drive up global oil prices; (2) internal U.S. policy deliberations about whether to discourage or support Ukraine's oil infrastructure strike campaign; and (3) communications with Ukraine regarding the same. The requested time period is January 1, 2024 through December 31, 2025.

BACKGROUND

Beginning in late 2023 and intensifying in 2024, Ukraine conducted a sustained campaign of long-range drone strikes against Russian oil refineries, fuel depots, and export terminals, targeting facilities operated by Rosneft, Lukoil, Surgutneftegas, and others. These strikes generated significant public tension between the United States and Ukraine, with the Biden administration reportedly discouraging the campaign due to concerns about the effect on global oil prices. Several European governments — particularly those dependent on refined petroleum products from Russia or sensitive to fuel price inflation — also expressed concern. The diplomatic communications underlying these public tensions are the subject of this request.

RECORDS SOUGHT

All diplomatic cables, telegrams, or electronic messages between the State Department and U.S. embassies or European partner governments — including but not limited to Germany, France, Italy, Poland, Hungary, Slovakia, and the European Commission — related to Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil and energy infrastructure, including any communications reflecting European concerns about oil price or supply impacts;
Internal State Department memoranda, options papers, or policy assessments addressing U.S. policy toward Ukraine's oil infrastructure strike campaign, including any deliberations over whether to formally or informally discourage Ukraine from continuing those strikes;
Communications between State Department officials — including Secretary Antony Blinken, the Bureau of European and Eurasian Affairs, and the Office of the Special Presidential Coordinator for Global Infrastructure and Energy Security (Ambassador Amos Hochstein) — and Ukrainian officials regarding the oil infrastructure strike campaign;
Any readouts, memoranda of conversation, or summaries of meetings between U.S. officials and European counterparts in which Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure were discussed;
Communications between the State Department and the National Security Council, National Economic Council, or Department of Energy regarding the diplomatic implications of Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure;
Any State Department assessments of the impact of the oil infrastructure strike campaign on U.S. diplomatic relationships with European partners or on the cohesion of the Western coalition supporting Ukraine;
Records related to any U.S. representations to the Ukrainian government — formal or informal — requesting restraint or modification of the oil infrastructure strike campaign.

NOTE ON CABLE TRAFFIC

Diplomatic cables responsive to this request may be withheld in part under Exemption 1 (classified) or Exemption 5 (deliberative process). I ask that the Department apply these exemptions narrowly and produce all reasonably segregable, unclassified portions. In particular, cables that reflect the positions of foreign governments on oil price or energy market concerns — as distinct from operational or intelligence content — are likely to be releasable in substantial part and should be produced accordingly.

KEY OFFICIALS

Relevant officials include: Secretary Antony Blinken and Secretary Marco Rubio, Ambassador Amos Hochstein, the Assistant Secretary for European and Eurasian Affairs, and the U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine. I ask that records custodians search email accounts, relevant cable systems, and shared drives associated with these officials for the requested period.
FEE WAIVER REQUEST

I request a waiver of all fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist engaged in research for a book on U.S. energy policy and the geopolitical dimensions of the Russia-Ukraine war. The diplomatic tensions between the United States, Ukraine, and European allies over the oil infrastructure strike campaign are a matter of significant public concern, bearing directly on the coherence of Western policy toward Ukraine and the relationship between military strategy and energy market stability. This request is not made for commercial purposes. If a full waiver is not granted, I request that fees be limited to duplication costs only, and that I be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred.

EXPEDITED PROCESSING REQUEST

I request expedited processing pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E) on the grounds of urgency to inform the public concerning actual or alleged federal government activity. Ukraine's campaign of long-range strikes on Russian energy infrastructure is an ongoing and consequential dimension of the war with direct implications for global energy markets and U.S. foreign policy. Timely disclosure will contribute directly to informed public debate about U.S. policy decisions in this area.

FORM OF PRODUCTION

Please produce all responsive records in electronic format (PDF or searchable text) via email or electronic delivery. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, I request a Vaughn index identifying each withheld record, the exemption(s) claimed, and a particularized explanation of how the exemption applies. I also request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of withheld records be released.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tim McDonnell

BACKGROUND

Beginning in late 2023 and significantly intensifying in early 2024, Ukraine conducted a sustained campaign of drone strikes against Russian oil refineries and export infrastructure. By mid-2024, publicly available analyses indicated that a substantial share of Russian refining capacity had been affected. These strikes raised questions about their effect on global diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel markets, on Russian crude export volumes, and on the functioning of the Russian oil price cap mechanism. The EIA's ongoing monitoring of global oil and refined product markets makes it a primary repository for unclassified analytical records on these questions.

BACKGROUND AND LEGAL BASIS

The Department of Defense is subject to several statutory reporting and notification obligations to Congress, including under the Arms Export Control Act, the Foreign Assistance Act, the National Security Act, and various annual National Defense Authorization Acts. Notifications made pursuant to these authorities — including those related to security assistance to Ukraine, assessments of Ukrainian military capabilities and operations, or assessments of the impact of those operations on regional stability — may include both classified and unclassified components. This request is limited to the unclassified components: cover letters, transmittal documents, unclassified summaries, and any portions of notifications that are not classified.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I hereby request copies of all unclassified records related to congressional notifications, cover letters, transmittal letters, or unclassified summaries provided by the Department of Defense — or any component thereof — to the United States Congress in connection with Ukrainian long-range drone strikes on Russian oil refining and energy export infrastructure. The requested time period is January 1, 2024 through December 31, 2025.

BACKGROUND AND LEGAL BASIS

The Department of Defense is subject to several statutory reporting and notification obligations to Congress, including under the Arms Export Control Act, the Foreign Assistance Act, the National Security Act, and various annual National Defense Authorization Acts. Notifications made pursuant to these authorities — including those related to security assistance to Ukraine, assessments of Ukrainian military capabilities and operations, or assessments of the impact of those operations on regional stability — may include both classified and unclassified components. This request is limited to the unclassified components: cover letters, transmittal documents, unclassified summaries, and any portions of notifications that are not classified.

RECORDS SOUGHT

All unclassified cover letters, transmittal letters, and unclassified summaries or abstracts accompanying any congressional notification provided by the Office of the Secretary of Defense, the Joint Chiefs of Staff, or any defense agency related to Ukrainian military operations against Russian energy, oil, or refining infrastructure;
Any unclassified portions of reports or notifications provided to the House and Senate Armed Services Committees, the House and Senate Foreign Relations/Foreign Affairs Committees, or the House and Senate Intelligence Committees related to Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure or the U.S. assessment of the impact of such strikes;
Unclassified portions of any DOD assessments provided to Congress regarding the effectiveness of Ukrainian long-range drone capabilities against Russian energy infrastructure, including any unclassified summary findings;
Any unclassified records reflecting DOD's assessment of the economic or strategic impact of Ukrainian oil infrastructure strikes on Russia's ability to fund or sustain its military operations;
Unclassified portions of any DOD records reflecting interagency deliberations — including with the State Department, NSC, or Treasury — regarding U.S. policy toward Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure, to the extent those deliberations were reflected in congressional notifications or related documents;
Any unclassified after-action assessments, lessons-learned documents, or public-facing briefing materials produced by DOD components regarding the Ukrainian oil infrastructure strike campaign.

SCOPE CLARIFICATION

I understand that the underlying classified congressional notifications are not subject to FOIA disclosure. This request is expressly limited to: (1) unclassified cover letters and transmittal documents accompanying classified notifications; (2) unclassified summaries or abstracts that may accompany or be appended to classified reports; and (3) any entirely unclassified notifications, assessments, or briefing materials related to this subject. I ask that DOD apply the principle of maximum segregability and produce all portions of responsive documents that do not themselves contain classified information, even where the parent document is classified.

KEY OFFICIALS AND OFFICES

Relevant offices include: the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Policy, the Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense for International Security Affairs (Europe and NATO), the Joint Staff (J5 Strategic Plans and Policy), and the Defense Intelligence Agency's energy and economic analysis components. I ask that records custodians search relevant document repositories, congressional correspondence tracking systems, and legislative affairs offices for the requested period.

FEE WAIVER REQUEST

I request a waiver of all fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist engaged in research for a book on U.S. energy policy and the geopolitical dimensions of the Russia-Ukraine war. The unclassified components of congressional notifications regarding Ukrainian military operations are precisely the category of records designed to maintain public accountability over executive branch activities. Disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of U.S. policy deliberations on a consequential dimension of the conflict. This request is not made for commercial purposes. If a full waiver is not granted, I request that fees be limited to duplication costs only, and that I be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred.

EXPEDITED PROCESSING REQUEST

I request expedited processing pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E). Ukraine's oil infrastructure strike campaign is an ongoing and evolving military and economic story with direct implications for U.S. policy and global energy markets. Timely disclosure of the unclassified record will contribute directly to informed public debate.

FORM OF PRODUCTION

Please produce all responsive records in electronic format (PDF or searchable text) via email or electronic delivery. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, I request a Vaughn index identifying each withheld record, the exemption(s) claimed, and a particularized explanation of how the exemption applies. I also request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of withheld records be released.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tim McDonnell

title: FOIA Request — Treasury Department Records: Economic Impact Analysis, Inflation Response, and Allied Coordination Related to Strait of Hormuz Disruption

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request — Treasury Department Records: Economic Impact Analysis, Inflation Response, and Allied Coordination Related to Strait of Hormuz Disruption
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I hereby request copies of all records in the possession of the Department of the Treasury — including the Office of International Affairs, the Office of Economic Policy, and the Office of Terrorism and Financial Intelligence — related to economic planning and analysis concerning potential or actual disruptions to oil and gas transit through the Strait of Hormuz, including records produced both before and after the closure of the Strait beginning in March 2026.

PART I — PRE-CONFLICT ECONOMIC ANALYSIS (January 1, 2024 – February 27, 2026)
I request all records within this period related to:

Internal Treasury analyses or scenario-planning documents assessing the macroeconomic and inflationary impact of a full or partial closure of the Strait of Hormuz on the U.S. economy;
Any Treasury communications with the Federal Reserve, Council of Economic Advisers, or OMB regarding energy price shock contingency planning related to a Hormuz disruption;
Records related to Treasury's participation in interagency processes — including NSC or NEC-led processes — addressing energy market risks associated with potential U.S. military action against Iran;
Any Treasury assessments of the interaction between potential Hormuz disruption scenarios and existing U.S. sanctions on Iranian oil exports.

PART II — CRISIS RESPONSE (February 28, 2026 – Present)
I request all records from the onset of military operations through the date of this request related to:

Internal Treasury analyses of the macroeconomic and inflationary impact of the Strait of Hormuz closure on the U.S. economy, including any projections of GDP impact, consumer price effects, or recession risk;
Communications between Treasury officials and the Federal Reserve regarding monetary policy implications of the oil price shock caused by the Hormuz closure;
Records related to Treasury's coordination with G7 finance ministries or the IEA on economic responses to the Hormuz closure, including burden-sharing arrangements for strategic reserve releases;
Any Treasury assessments of the adequacy of SPR drawdown as an inflation mitigation tool, or consideration of additional fiscal or regulatory responses to energy price increases;
Records related to Treasury's role in any discussions of sanctions modifications or waivers that arose in the context of the Hormuz closure and global oil supply concerns.

KEY OFFICIALS

Relevant officials include: Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent, the Under Secretary for International Affairs, and the Assistant Secretary for Economic Policy. Records custodians should search email accounts and relevant policy workspaces for the requested period.

FEE WAIVER REQUEST

I request a waiver of all fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist engaged in research for a book on U.S. energy policy and the geopolitical dimensions of the Iran conflict. Disclosure of the requested records will contribute significantly to public understanding of how the U.S. government planned for and responded to one of the most consequential energy supply disruptions in recent history. This request is not made for commercial purposes. If a full waiver is not granted, I request that fees be limited to duplication costs only, and that I be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred.

EXPEDITED PROCESSING REQUEST

I request expedited processing pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E). The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is an ongoing crisis with immediate and severe consequences for U.S. energy markets, inflation, and foreign policy. Timely disclosure of the requested records will contribute directly to urgent public debate about how the U.S. government prepared for and is managing this crisis.

FORM OF PRODUCTION

Please produce all responsive records in electronic format (PDF or searchable text) via email or electronic delivery. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, I request a Vaughn index identifying each withheld record, the exemption(s) claimed, and a particularized explanation of how the exemption applies. I also request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of withheld records be released.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tim McDonnell

title: Flock  and Similar Device Usage

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Flock  and Similar Device Usage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act (S.C. Code § 30-4-10 et seq.), I hereby request copies of the following public records:

1. Flock Safety Devices & Similar Technologies
All contracts, purchase orders, lease agreements, or memoranda of understanding with Flock Safety or any vendor providing automated license plate readers (ALPRs), AI-assisted vehicle recognition, or similar passive surveillance infrastructure deployed within Charleston County limits including but not limited to the City of Charleston, Mt. Pleasant, Sullivans Island, Isle of Palms, James Island, Folly Beach, Johns Island and North Charleston. 

2. Data Collection Practices
All records describing or governing what data is collected by Flock Safety devices or similar AI-assisted surveillance systems, including but not limited to:

	•	Vehicle license plates, make, model, and color
	•	Driver or occupant imagery
	•	Location, time, and frequency of vehicle sightings
	•	Any biometric or behavioral data captured incidentally

3. Data Storage & Retention
All records detailing how collected surveillance data is stored, including:

	•	Storage location (local servers, cloud, third-party)
	•	Retention periods for individual records
	•	Data security policies and breach protocols
	•	Whether Flock Safety or any vendor retains independent access to collected data

4. Profile Creation & Pattern Analysis
All records, policies, or technical documentation describing whether collected data is used to:

	•	Build profiles of individuals based on travel patterns, routes, or frequency of movement
	•	Generate alerts or flags based on behavioral patterns or location history
	•	Share individual or aggregated movement data with other agencies, fusion centers, or federal entities

5. Access & Oversight
All records identifying:

	•	Which personnel or agencies have access to surveillance data
	•	Any third-party data sharing agreements, including law enforcement at other jurisdictions
	•	Audit logs or oversight mechanisms governing data access and use

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

I request a fee waiver on the basis that this request serves the public interest in government transparency and accountability.

Thank you for your timely response as required under SC FOIA.

title: Obama Presidential Library: White House Counsel Inter-Agency Records Concerning Jens Söring (2010–2014)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all electronic communications (including emails, email attachments, cables, and calendar invites), legal memoranda, briefsheets, and records of communication sent or received by the Office of the White House Counsel (WHCO) between January 1, 2010, and December 31, 2014, regarding the proposed repatriation, international treaty transfer, parole, or clemency of Virginia Department of Corrections prisoner Jens Söring (Soering).

Jens Söring was involved in a murder case which generated much friction between the governments of the United States and Germany. A recent Netflix series called "Till Murder Do Us Part: Soering vs. Haysom" claimed that the case resulted in the highest level involvement of German officials in advocating the Obama administration to transfer Söring to Germany. This request, filed in the public interest, aims to illuminate what happened for history's benefit. 

Specifically, this request seeks communications between staff members of the Office of the White House Counsel and representatives of the following external agencies/components:

The U.S. Department of State: Specifically the Office of the Legal Adviser (L), including the Office of Law Enforcement and Intelligence (L/LEI) and the Office of European and Eurasian Affairs (L/EUR).

The U.S. Department of Justice: Specifically the Criminal Division’s Office of International Affairs (OIA) and the Office of Legal Counsel (OLC).

The Commonwealth of Virginia: Specifically the Office of the Governor (under Governors Tim Kaine and Bob McDonnell) and the Virginia Department of Corrections (VADOC).

Targeted Record Custodians

To facilitate an efficient search of the Electronic Records of the President, please prioritize the digital files, email accounts, and archives of the following specific officials within the Office of the White House Counsel during the specified timeframe of January 1 2010 to December 31 2014. Please search all electronic records, including emails and email attachments, associated with the accounts, PST files, archived mailboxes, and electronic records of the following individuals:

Robert F. Bauer (White House Counsel)

Kathryn Ruemmler (Principal Deputy Counsel / White House Counsel)

Neil Eggleston (White House Counsel)

Daniel J. Meltzer (Principal Deputy Counsel)

Danielle Gray (Associate Counsel to the President)

Christopher Fonzone (Associate Counsel / NSC Legal Advisor)

Nicholas McQuaid (Associate Counsel)

Suggested Search Methodology & Keywords

To locate these records within the electronic archive, I request that your office execute search queries utilizing the following terms and Boolean configurations:

Primary Keywords: "Jens Söring" OR "Jens Soering" OR "Jens Sorin" OR "Söring" OR "Soering" OR "Haysom"

Contextual Terms: "repatriation" OR "prisoner transfer" OR "treaty transfer" OR "parole" OR "clemency" OR "conditional approval" OR "Council of Europe"

Jurisdictional Terms: "Governor Kaine" OR "Tim Kaine" OR "Governor McDonnell" OR "Bob McDonnell" OR "Virginia Department of Corrections" OR "VADOC" OR "German Embassy" OR "Diplomatic Note"

If certain parts of this request or if certain portions must be redacted, please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. In plain English: please weigh on the side of showing, line-by-line, exactly what each exemption is. 

Thank you for the work that the Obama Presidential Library does and for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Flock  and Similar Device Usage (City of Charleston)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act (S.C. Code § 30-4-10 et seq.), I hereby request copies of the following public records:

1. Flock Safety Devices & Similar Technologies
All contracts, purchase orders, lease agreements, or memoranda of understanding with Flock Safety or any vendor providing automated license plate readers (ALPRs), AI-assisted vehicle recognition, or similar passive surveillance infrastructure deployed within Charleston County limits including but not limited to the City of Charleston, Mt. Pleasant, Sullivans Island, Isle of Palms, James Island, Folly Beach, Johns Island and North Charleston. 

2. Data Collection Practices
All records describing or governing what data is collected by Flock Safety devices or similar AI-assisted surveillance systems, including but not limited to:

	•	Vehicle license plates, make, model, and color
	•	Driver or occupant imagery
	•	Location, time, and frequency of vehicle sightings
	•	Any biometric or behavioral data captured incidentally

3. Data Storage & Retention
All records detailing how collected surveillance data is stored, including:

	•	Storage location (local servers, cloud, third-party)
	•	Retention periods for individual records
	•	Data security policies and breach protocols
	•	Whether Flock Safety or any vendor retains independent access to collected data

4. Profile Creation & Pattern Analysis
All records, policies, or technical documentation describing whether collected data is used to:

	•	Build profiles of individuals based on travel patterns, routes, or frequency of movement
	•	Generate alerts or flags based on behavioral patterns or location history
	•	Share individual or aggregated movement data with other agencies, fusion centers, or federal entities

5. Access & Oversight
All records identifying:

	•	Which personnel or agencies have access to surveillance data
	•	Any third-party data sharing agreements, including law enforcement at other jurisdictions
	•	Audit logs or oversight mechanisms governing data access and use

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

I request a fee waiver on the basis that this request serves the public interest in government transparency and accountability.

Thank you for your timely response as required under SC FOIA.

Charges:            attempted murder of a police officer, resisting arrest, driving under the influence (third offense)
Officers involved:Chris Siglin

RECORDS REQUESTED:

1. ALL body-worn camera (BWC) footage from each of the following officers and any additional officers who responded to, participated in, or were present during this incident:
